data.body: ROOT CAUSE of claim staleness (corrected): NOT the overlap enrich drain. It's the plan-review GROUNDING detector: review-plan computes the material fingerprint at pass1-start, then its grounding step auto-corrects file_impact (it added src/rebar/schemas/gen_types.py to 6fe2's file_impact 14->15, exactly the E6 indeterminate finding), then signs the PRE-grounding fingerprint -> stale-on-arrival. This self-heals: once file_impact has converged to the grounded-correct set, a subsequent review signs the matching fingerprint. Re-reviewing epic + 6fe2 now (both file_impact converged) then claiming. r4 6fe2 review was PASS 0-blocking (after remediating the Jira-seam scope contradiction). Also remediated: 6fe2 desc no longer claims the Jira CREATE seam calls validate_creation_channel (that's e622).
